query: Can you suggest some recent datasets that have been used for studying stance detection in tweets, particularly those targeting specific individuals and events since 2020?
criteria: stance detection tweet datasets individuals events 2020
recent stance detection datasets tweets specific person event

title.value: MAWQIF: A Multi-label Arabic Dataset for Target-specific Stance Detection

recommendation_reason.value: MAWQIF, the first Arabic dataset for target-specific stance detection, composed of 4,121 tweets annotated with stance, sentiment, and sarcasm polarities.

supported_research_question.value: Stance detection determines the viewpoint expressed in a text toward a target.

method.value: We provide a detailed description of the dataset, present an analysis of the produced annotation, and evaluate four BERT-based models on it.

data_or_scenario.value: composed of 4,121 tweets annotated with stance, sentiment, and sarcasm polarities

main_conclusion.value: Our best model achieves a macro-F 1 of 78.89%, which shows that there is ample room for improvement on this challenging task.

limitations.value: Although some efforts have recently been made to develop stance detection datasets in other languages, no similar efforts seem to have considered the Arabic language.

relevance_strength.value: first Arabic dataset for target-specific stance detection

title.value: A Few Topical Tweets are Enough for Effective User Stance Detection

recommendation_reason.value: User stance detection entails ascertaining the position of a user towards a target, such as an entity, topic, or claim.

supported_research_question.value: User stance detection entails ascertaining the position of a user towards a target, such as an entity, topic, or claim.

method.value: improve user-level stance detection by representing tweets using contextualized embeddings, which capture latent meanings of words in context

main_conclusion.value: This approach achieves 95.6% accuracy and 93.1% macro F-measure.

limitations.value: perform poorly or fail completely for less vocal users, who may have authored only a few tweets about a target

relevance_strength.value: User stance detection entails ascertaining the position of a user towards a target, such as an entity, topic, or claim.

title.value: P-Stance: A Large Dataset for Stance Detection in Political Domain

recommendation_reason.value: a large stance detection dataset in the political domain, which contains 21,574 labeled tweets.

supported_research_question.value: Stance detection determines whether the author of a text is in favor of, against or neutral to a specific target and provides valuable insights into important events such as presidential election.

method.value: We provide a detailed description of the newly created dataset and develop deep learning models on it. Our best model achieves a macro-average F1-score of 80.53%, which we improve further by using semi-supervised learning.

data_or_scenario.value: P-STANCE, a large stance detection dataset in the political domain, which contains 21,574 labeled tweets.

main_conclusion.value: Our best model achieves a macro-average F1-score of 80.53%, which we improve further by using semi-supervised learning. Moreover, our P-STANCE dataset can facilitate research in the fields of cross-domain stance detection such as cross-target stance detection where a classifier is adapted from a different but related target.

limitations.value: However, progress on stance detection has been hampered by the absence of large annotated datasets.
